item.title: The Prevalence of Fibromyalgia in the General Population and At-Risk Subpopulations: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis.
item.description: Anesthesia and analgesia - Estimates of fibromyalgia prevalence vary widely worldwide due to differences in diagnostic criteria, study methodology, and population characteristics. Accurate estimates are essential to inform p...

item.title: Berberine Improved the Therapeutic Efficacy of UC-MSCs for DSS-Induced Colitis via Aerobic Glycolysis.
item.description: Phytotherapy research : PTR - The global prevalence of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) continues increasing, but effective targeted treatments remain elusive. Recently, mesenchymal stem cells (MSCs) have emerged as a promising...

item.title: Inflammatory Bowel Disease Is Associated With Persistent Functional Impairment and Increased Short- and Long-Term Complications Following Total Knee Arthroplasty.
item.description: Orthopedic reviews - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is increasingly prevalent, and more affected patients are undergoing total knee arthroplasty (TKA). However, the effect of comorbid IBD on short- and long-term post...

item.title: Computer-Assisted and Unassisted Optical Diagnosis of Colorectal Polyps: Results from a Prospective Pragmatic Implementation Study.
item.description: Endoscopy - Background and Aims Computer-aided diagnosis (CADx) may support optical diagnosis of diminutive (≤5 mm) colorectal polyps, replacing histopathology. We aimed to describe CADx usage and diagnostic p...

item.title: Evaluation of the Anti-Inflammatory Activity of Selected Plant Extracts in an In Vitro Model of Inflammation Using LPS-Stimulated Macrophages.
item.description: Biomedicines - Background: Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), including Crohn's disease (CD) and ulcerative colitis (UC), is a group of chronic gastrointestinal (GI) diseases with complex and multifactorial pathop...

item.title: Association between nutritional status at admission and inadequate bowel preparation in inflammatory bowel disease patients: a retrospective cohort study.
item.description: Frontiers in nutrition - Malnutrition is highly prevalent among patients with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). However, the relationship between nutritional status and the quality of bowel preparation in this population r...
